notes: Sourced from PostgreSQL 17 chapter 9 sections: 9.3 (math), 9.4 (string), 9.5 (bytea), 9.6 (bitstring), 9.7 (regex covered under string), 9.8 (formatting), 9.9 (datetime), 9.11 (network), 9.12 (text search), 9.16 (json), 9.18 (conditional), 9.19 (array), 9.20 (range), 9.21 (aggregate), 9.22 (window), 9.23 (subscripting/SRF), 9.25 (system info), 9.26 (system admin partially - only query-time utility), comparison (9.2). Skipped: pg_catalog admin functions (replication, pg_stat_*, pg_size_*, lo_*, large object, advisory locks, pgstattuple, etc.), trigger/event-trigger SRFs, XML functions (omitted - rarely used), uuid-ossp module functions (not core). Operators excluded per spec. Aliases collapsed to canonical entry. Date assumes PG17. Set-returning forms tagged where applicable.

notes: Source: MySQL 9.7 Reference Manual built-in-function-reference.html. Operators (+, -, *, /, %, ||, &&, !, <, >, =, <=>, IS, IS NULL, LIKE, REGEXP, BETWEEN, IN, EXISTS, BINARY cast keyword, etc.) excluded per scope. Procedural / replication-control / internal (CAN_ACCESS_*, INTERNAL_*) functions excluded as procedural extensions. Aliases such as CHARACTER_LENGTH=CHAR_LENGTH, CEILING=CEIL, POWER=POW, UCASE=UPPER, LCASE=LOWER, OCTET_LENGTH=LENGTH, POSITION=LOCATE, SUBSTR=SUBSTRING, MID=SUBSTRING, DAY=DAYOFMONTH, SCHEMA=DATABASE, SESSION_USER/SYSTEM_USER=USER, CURRENT_DATE=CURDATE, CURRENT_TIME=CURTIME, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP/LOCALTIME/LOCALTIMESTAMP=NOW, STD/STDDEV=STDDEV_POP, VARIANCE=VAR_POP, ST_AsWKT=ST_AsText, ST_AsWKB=ST_AsBinary, ST_GeomFromText=ST_GeometryFromText, etc., collapsed into canonical entries' aliases. MD5(), SHA1()/SHA() retained even though docs page recommends SHA2(): they remain available built-ins in 9.x. Vector (DISTANCE, STRING_TO_VECTOR, VECTOR_DIM, VECTOR_TO_STRING) included from MySQL 9 vector additions. Locking, replication helper, performance-schema, GTID-wait functions included as system functions. XML, GIS (ST_*), JSON, window functions all included.

notes: Focus per scope: MariaDB-specific extras over MySQL (sequence functions, dynamic columns, vector functions, UUID v4/v7/SHORT, Galera WSREP, Oracle-compat NVL/NVL2/DECODE_ORACLE/ADD_MONTHS/MONTHS_BETWEEN/TO_CHAR/TO_NUMBER/TRIM_ORACLE/ROWNUM/SYS_GUID, JSON_VALID/JSON_SCHEMA_VALID/JSON_NORMALIZE/JSON_DETAILED/JSON_KEY_VALUE/JSON_OBJECT_FILTER_KEYS/JSON_OBJECT_TO_ARRAY/JSON_ARRAY_INTERSECT/JSON_COMPACT/JSON_LOOSE/JSON_EQUALS, full REGEXP_* set, SFORMAT, NATURAL_SORT_KEY, CRC32C, KDF, RANDOM_BYTES, FORMAT_BYTES). Also includes the common-with-MySQL core for completeness so gap-analysis can dedupe. Aliases noted on canonical entries (RLIKE on REGEXP, SUBSTR/MID on SUBSTRING, etc.). Geographic ST_* functions partially enumerated -- docs.mariadb.com geographic-functions subpages returned mostly index stubs; full ST_* surface (~80+ functions) is well known but not fully re-listed here -- I included the representative core (constructors, properties, set ops, relations, format conversion) plus MariaDB-specific spatial extensions (BUFFER, CONVEXHULL, PointOnSurface, ST_AsGeoJSON, ST_GeomFromGeoJSON). Skipped operators (+, -, ||, AND, OR, BETWEEN, IN, IS NULL, etc.) and procedural extensions (stored procedures, triggers). Version assumptions: MariaDB 11.x as of 2026 (JSON_ARRAY_INTERSECT, JSON_KEY_VALUE, JSON_OBJECT_FILTER_KEYS, JSON_OBJECT_TO_ARRAY are 11.2+; JSON_SCHEMA_VALID is 11.1+; UUID_v4/UUID_v7 are 11.x; vector functions are 11.7+; JSON_OVERLAPS is 10.9+).

notes: Sourced from DuckDB current docs (https://duckdb.org/docs/current/sql/functions/...). Walked 17 category pages: overview, text, numeric, date, timestamp, timestamptz, interval, time, aggregates, list, struct, map, array, regular_expressions, pattern_matching, utility, window_functions, bitstring, blob, enum, union, geometry, lambda. Skipped operators (||, +, -, *, /, %, [], LIKE/SIMILAR TO operator forms) per instructions. Geometry/spatial section enumerates only the built-in (non-extension) geometry functions; the full spatial extension adds many more ST_* functions not documented on this page. ICU extension timestamptz functions (age, date_diff, etc.) are merged into the main datetime entries since names overlap. Aliases consolidated into 'aliases' field where multiple names share a canonical implementation (e.g. array_*/list_* duplicates from list page collapsed). Bitstring aggregates (bit_and/or/xor, bitstring_agg) deduped with their numeric/aggregate counterparts. read_text and read_blob appear in both text and utility pages; classified once as 'file'. glob and repeat_row are table functions but included as commonly user-facing. Lambda page contains no new functions beyond list_transform/filter/reduce and their aliases.

Inventory compiled from ClickHouse documentation across category index pages: arithmetic, comparison, logical, conditional, string, string-search, string-replace, array, date-time, math, type-conversion, json, url, ip-address, hash, bit, encoding, uuid, geo (coordinates, geohash, h3, s2), tuple, map, distance, nlp, time-series, time-window, splitting/merging, dictionaries, random, rounding, bitmap, null, ml, files, aggregate-functions/reference, window-functions, and other. Includes ~140 aggregate functions; combinators (-If, -Array, -State, -Merge, etc) noted but not enumerated separately. Many toXxx date extraction functions (toYear, toMonth, toDayOfMonth, toHour, toMinute, toSecond, toDayOfYear, toDayOfWeek, toWeek, toQuarter, toUnixTimestamp, toStartOfXxx, toRelativeXxxNum, toYYYYMM, etc) included as they are core to date-time category. ClickHouse documents 1500+ functions overall; this inventory captures the canonical ones across the main category index pages. Operators (+, *, ||) skipped per scope. Some highly experimental or extremely niche functions may be missing.

notes: Consolidated inventory of canonical built-in SQL functions across Snowflake and BigQuery, two major cloud analytical engines. Functions present in both engines are recorded once with notes on naming differences. Distinct functions are kept separate. Snowflake APPROX_PERCENTILE corresponds to BigQuery APPROX_QUANTILES (different signatures - listed separately). Snowflake EDITDISTANCE vs BigQuery EDIT_DISTANCE - listed under canonical EDIT_DISTANCE with alias. Snowflake uses CHARINDEX/POSITION/STRPOS variants; BigQuery uses STRPOS/INSTR. Snowflake uses CHAR/CHR; BigQuery uses CHR. Both have H3 (Snowflake) vs S2 (BigQuery) spatial grids - both retained. Procedural extensions (CALL, EXECUTE IMMEDIATE), table-only metadata functions (e.g., COPY_HISTORY, DATABASE_REFRESH_HISTORY system tables), operators (||, ::, +), and CASE/WHEN syntax were skipped. The Snowflake docs page summary was truncated mid-list (H-Z entries summarized as a single line); included known H-Z canonical entries from Snowflake reference based on documented function inventory. AI/Cortex LLM-style functions and ML.PREDICT-style functions exist in both but were partially included as 'other' category given they are vendor-specific. BigQuery DLP/AEAD encryption functions retained as 'other'. Differentially private aggregate variants from both engines noted but consolidated where overlapping. Some Snowflake table-function metadata helpers (DATA_METRIC_*, DATABASE_REFRESH_*, etc.) included as 'system' rather than skipped because they are documented as functions; users interested in pure scalar/aggregate inventory should filter to non-'system' categories.
